revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:令外官]][[zh:令外官]]

'''Ryōge no Kan''' (令外官) refers to {{ill|East Asian government position|lt=government positions|ja|官職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Asian government position|qq=}} established outside the framework of the {{ill|Traditional Chinese law|en|Traditional Chinese law|qid=Q4380197|lt=Ritsuryō|1=Traditional Chinese law}} system. The government offices overseen by these officials are sometimes referred to as '''Ryōge no Kanshi''' (令外官司).

These positions were created to address practical political issues flexibly and responsively, without being constrained by the existing {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|qid=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}} or {{ill|Japanese administrative structure|ja|日本の官制|1=structure of the ritsuryō government|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese administrative structure|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese administrative system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8193883}}. Originating in China, many Ryōge no Kan were established during the early to mid-8th century. In Japan, numerous such positions were introduced during the reforms of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|qid=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}} in the late 8th century, and additional posts continued to be created as needed.

== Ryōge no Kan in Tang China ==

The {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|qid=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty|1=Tang dynasty}}'s Ritsuryō laws were highly comprehensive, consolidating ancient governmental positions and legal codes. However, this perfection also resulted in a coexistence of idealistic administrative roles and practically necessary offices, some of which had overlapping duties. Despite these contradictions, no significant reforms were made to the administrative system in early Tang.

In the late 7th century, {{ill|Wu Zetian|en|Wu Zetian|qid=Q9738|lt=Wu Zetian|1=Wu Zetian}}, having risen to power as an empress, faced opposition from the aristocracy. To secure her authority and promote talented {{ill|Imperial examination|en|Imperial examination|qid=Q7408|lt=imperial examination|1=Imperial examination}} officials, she created positions outside the legal framework, known as {{ill|Yuanwaikan|ja|員外官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuanwaikan|1=Yuanwaikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55521279}}, greatly increasing the number of officials. The Ritsuryō, being rooted in {{ill|Confucianism|en|Confucianism|qid=Q9581|lt=Confucianism|1=Confucianism}}, was considered ideal and thus resistant to modifications (Miyazaki Ichisada, "Jibatsushū," p.187, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|en|Iwanami Shoten|qid=Q1331427|lt=Iwanami Shoten|1=Iwanami Shoten}}).

In the early 8th century, {{ill|Emperor Xuanzong of Tang|en|Emperor Xuanzong of Tang|qid=Q9746|lt=Emperor Xuanzong of Tang|1=Emperor Xuanzong of Tang}} reversed Wu Zetian’s expansion of official posts, striving to control their growth. Nevertheless, societal realities had diverged significantly from the Ritsuryō framework, necessitating new offices. As a result, positions outside the Ritsuryō—Ryōge no Kan—were increasingly established.

Many of these roles were termed "○○shi" (使), collectively referred to as {{ill|Shishoku|ja|使職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, contrasting with traditional Ritsuryō positions.

* '''Military Affairs:''' The obsolete {{ill|Fubing system|en|Fubing system|qid=Q2501249|lt=Fubing system|1=Fubing system}} was replaced by a recruitment-based military system, leading to the establishment of positions like {{ill|Tuanlianshi|ja|団練使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tuanlianshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Jiedushi|en|Jiedushi|qid=Q1688932|lt=Jiedushi|1=Jiedushi}}.
* '''Fiscal Administration:''' To support growing military expenditures, offices like {{ill|Duzhishi|ja|度支使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Duzhishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Salt and Iron Commissioner|ja|塩鉄使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Salt and Iron Commissioner|1=Salt and Iron Commissioner|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q109359832}}, {{ill|Soyōshi|ja|租庸使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soyōshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Zhuanyunshi|ja|転運使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhuanyunshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Water-Land Transport Commissioner|ja|水陸運使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Water-Land Transport Commissioner|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were created.
* '''Administrative Oversight:''' Positions like {{ill|Guanchashi|ja|観察使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guanchashi|1=Gwanchalsa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8011297}}, {{ill|Anzhishi|ja|按察使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anzhishi|1=Provincial Judge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q848663}}, and {{ill|Caifangshi|ja|採訪使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Caifangshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 採訪処置使|1=Caifangshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11498266}} were established for monitoring governance.

These positions gradually overshadowed traditional Ritsuryō roles, leading to bureaucratic bloat and the inclusion of many civilians as {{ill|Shori (clerks)|ja|胥吏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shori (clerks)|1=li|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5371955}}. Unlike Ritsuryō officials, whose appointments required imperial approval, Ryōge no Kan could appoint subordinates at their discretion, enabling wealthy landowners to secure official titles, thus contributing to the rise of the {{ill|Scholar-official|en|Scholar-official|qid=Q708299|lt=Scholar-official|1=Scholar-official}} class in the late Tang.

== Ryōge no Kan in Japan ==

In Japan, Ryōge no Kan were introduced shortly after the establishment of the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|qid=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code|1=Taihō Code}}. Early examples include {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|qid=Q1133663|lt=Sangi|1=Sangi (Japan)}}, the {{ill|Zōheijōkyōshi|ja|造平城京使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōheijōkyōshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|qid=Q1185382|lt=Chūnagon|1=Chūnagon}}, and {{ill|Azechi|ja|按察使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azechi|1=Provincial Judge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q848663}}.

To cultivate officials versed in non-Confucian knowledge, posts like {{ill|Monjō Hakase|ja|文章博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjō Hakase|1=monjō hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11500074}} and {{ill|Myōhō Hakase|ja|明法博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō Hakase|qq=}} were established as Ryōge no Kan within the {{ill|Daigaku-ryō|en|Daigaku-ryō|qid=Q3012048|lt=Daigaku-ryō|1=Daigaku-ryō}}, with Monjō Hakase later becoming its chief academic title.

During the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|qid=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin|1=Emperor Junnin}} and {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|qid=Q232955|lt=Empress Kōken|1=Empress Kōken}} (later {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Empress Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q232955}}), numerous Ryōge no Kan, such as the {{ill|Zōgūshō|ja|造宮省|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōgūshō|1=Zōgūshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11639741}}, {{ill|Chokushishō|ja|勅旨省|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishō|1=Chokushishō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11399930}}, {{ill|Naijusho|ja|内豎省|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naijusho|1=Naijusho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11394598}}, and {{ill|Hōōkyūshoku|ja|法王宮職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōōkyūshoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, proliferated, causing political instability.

By the late 8th century, as the Ritsuryō system weakened, {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|qid=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}} initiated major administrative reforms, leading to the proactive establishment of Ryōge no Kan to compensate for Ritsuryō deficiencies.

* In '''797''', the {{ill|Kageyushi|ja|勘解由使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kageyushi|1=Kageyushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10901369}} was established to oversee provincial administration.
* In '''810''', {{ill|Emperor Saga|en|Emperor Saga|qid=Q316654|lt=Emperor Saga|1=Emperor Saga}} founded the {{ill|Kurōdodokoro|ja|蔵人所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurōdodokoro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵人|1=Kurōdo-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6447696}} (headed by {{ill|Kurōdō no Tō|ja|蔵人頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurōdō no Tō|1=Kurōdonotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11621873}}) as a secretarial office handling imperial documents.
* In '''824''', to maintain order in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q740246}}, he established the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|qq=}}.

As local wealth accumulated in the 10th century, disputes over land ownership and deteriorating security prompted the creation of offices like {{ill|Ōryōshi|ja|押領使|1=ōryōshi|2=ja|3=押領使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōryōshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11497733}} and {{ill|Tsuisōshi|ja|追捕使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuisōshi|1=tsuibushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11639275}} to maintain law and order.

Additionally, positions such as {{ill|Kampaku|ja|関白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kampaku|1=kampaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1199966}} (established in '''884'''), {{ill|Regent|en|Regent|qid=Q477406|lt=Sesshō|1=Regent}}, and {{ill|Nairan|en|Nairan|qid=Q5365623|lt=Naiken|1=Nairan}} were Ryōge no Kan. From the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|Fujiwara Hok-ke|en|Fujiwara Hok-ke|qid=Q2975268|lt=Fujiwara Hokke|1=Fujiwara Hok-ke}} dominated these roles, ushering in the era of {{ill|Sekkan politics|ja|摂関政治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan politics|1=Sekkan Seiji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q588543}}.

The {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|qid=Q131767|lt=Sei-i Taishōgun|1=Shogun}}, later the de facto head of the {{ill|Bakufu|ja|武家政権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakufu|1=samurai government|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11545907}}, also originated as a Ryōge no Kan, initially intended for subjugating the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|qid=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}}.

=== Major Ryōge no Kan ===

* {{ill|Kampaku|ja|関白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kampaku|1=kampaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1199966}}
* {{ill|Naidaijin|en|Naidaijin|qid=Q1185338|lt=Naidaijin|1=Naidaijin}}
* {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|qid=Q1185382|lt=Chūnagon|1=Chūnagon}}
* {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|qid=Q1133663|lt=Sangi|1=Sangi (Japan)}}
* {{ill|Kurōdo-dokoro|en|Kurōdo-dokoro|qid=Q6447696|lt=Kurōdō|1=Kurōdo-dokoro}} ({{ill|Kurōdō no Tō|ja|蔵人頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurōdō no Tō|1=Kurōdonotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11621873}})
* {{ill|Monjō Hakase|ja|文章博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjō Hakase|1=monjō hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11500074}}
* {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|qid=Q131767|lt=Sei-i Taishōgun|1=Shogun}}
* {{ill|Chinjufu shōgun|en|Chinjufu shōgun|qid=Q2700052|lt=Chinjufu Shōgun|1=Chinjufu shōgun}}
* {{ill|Kageyushi|ja|勘解由使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kageyushi|1=Kageyushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10901369}}
* {{ill|Mushadokoro|ja|武者所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mushadokoro|1=Mushadokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17217077}}
* {{ill|Zassho Ketsudansho|ja|雑訴決断所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zassho Ketsudansho|1=Zasso Ketsudansho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11659416}}
* {{ill|Kiroku Shōen Kenkeisho|ja|記録荘園券契所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiroku Shōen Kenkeisho|1=Kiroku Shōen Kenkeisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11631142}}
* {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|qq=}}
* {{ill|Ōryōshi|ja|押領使|1=ōryōshi|2=ja|3=押領使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōryōshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11497733}}
* {{ill|Tsuisōshi|ja|追捕使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuisōshi|1=tsuibushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11639275}}
* {{ill|Myōhō Hakase|ja|明法博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōhō Hakase|qq=}}

{{translated page|ja|令外官|version=101096017|comment=Imported full ja history}}
